title: TDS Operations Analyst II
description: **Work Location** : Dublin, Ireland ****Hours:**** 35 ****Line Of Business:**** TD Securities ****Pay Details:**** We’re committed to providing fair and equitable compensation to all our colleagues. As a candidate, we encourage you to have an open dialogue with a member of our HR Team and ask compensation related questions, including pay details for this role. ****Job Description:**** **Position Title:** TDS Operations Analyst, Client Enablement **Level:** L07 ****Global Operations \& Business Services Overview:**** The Global Operations and Business Services team is a diverse and dynamic group of 850\+ people delivering continued business success through leadership, collaboration, innovation and forward\-thinking. We provide critical infrastructure services that support and/or control the trading, investment and corporate banking functions of TD Securities. Within our core values, we champion a culture of diversity, inclusion and community support/giving. ****Department Overview:**** Our team believes the Bank of the future will be driven by customer centricity. By understanding and meeting the needs of our customers, TD Securities will set itself apart as the Bank that truly "thinks customer". The Client Enablement team is responsible for managing, analyzing, and updating client, credit, and settlement data across TD Securities trading and credit risk systems. We are a global team that embraces and leverages data in a transformation manner while raising standards in data management through technology, governance, and processes that deliver sustainable results. We are a diverse and talented team that provides an integral core function to the business and your role is a key part of our success. By understanding the needs of our business partners, our purpose is to deliver complete and accurate data integrity. Our team lives on TD's Shared Commitments; we execute with speed and impact, innovate with purpose, act like an owner, think like a customer and develop our colleagues. ****Job Description:**** We are seeking a driven and analytical team member. In this role, you will play a pivotal part in supporting the bank's strategic mandate by ensuring the integrity and efficiency of our trading, settlement, and credit risk operations. As a key data owner for several business\-as\-usual processes, you will be responsible for maintaining critical data flows, validating information, and supporting seamless operational execution across multiple teams. Are you a professional who has a passion for data and can work collaboratively in a dynamic and evolving work environment? Do you want to build the foundation to a successful career while being part of the better bank? If so, a career at TDS Client Enablement may be the right fit for you. **In This Role, You Will:** * Enable clients in TD Securities book of record systems to facilitate trading and settlement. Own and manage business as usual processes associated with these systems, ensuring accuracy and alignment with operational needs of the business. * Act as an SME for Client Static and SSI Data across multiple TDS Platforms, establishing a deep understanding of end to end product set up * Lead daily operational tasks, ensuring timely and accurate data validation, data entry, reconciliation, and reporting. * Understand and maintain automated solutions and bulk data setup capabilities that are utilized to meet the growing demands of the business and perform critical data integrity checks across systems. Analyze and resolve exceptions from various reconciliation processes and automations. * Achieve an exceptional standard of error free processing by ensuring accurate inputting of data to mitigate risk to TD (e.g., reputational, financial, regulatory, operational risk) * Respond and resolve customers' concerns using standard procedures and escalating non\-standard activities, as necessary. * Collaborate with internal stakeholders, including senior leadership and Sales and Trading to review trade settlements, client reporting, and compliance activities and challenges. Act as a first line of support for the team with Audit and Risk Review activities. * Play a key role in identifying and resolving operational risks through careful data analysis and proactive troubleshooting. * Continuously identify and implement improvements in existing processes to drive efficiency and effectiveness within the operations function. * Act as a lead for testing, verification, and implementation of system updates and enhancements to ensure alignment with the Bank’s operational strategy. * Undertake project tasks as required and ensuring project deadlines are met. * Help build a positive, diverse and equitable work environment by promoting team effectiveness. * Demonstrate TD Framework and shared commitments in daily activities. ****Skills And Qualifications:**** * University or college degree or relevant experience * Ability to connect day\-to\-day operational activities with broader business objectives. * High accuracy in managing data and executing daily and repetitive operational tasks, ensuring operational excellence. * Ability to clearly articulate issues and solutions with internal stakeholders, fostering collaboration across departments. * Foundational knowledge of Capital Markets operations and how various data points flow through interconnected systems is an asset * A self\-starter with a proactive approach to learning and driving improvements, eager to contribute to broader strategic goals * Proficiency in data management tools (e.g., Excel, SQL), with a strong capacity to analyze data, identify trends, and highlight discrepancies is an asset * Familiarity with robotic process automation (RPA) such as UI Path and Alteryx, and an ability to assist in system testing and data reconciliation processes is preferred. * Experience or understanding of operational risk and process improvement initiatives are an asset. * Demonstrate strong organizational, planning and time management skills. * Ability to deliver legendary service while adhering to strict deadlines in a fast\-paced work environment. * Prior client setup and SSI knowledge is an asset ****Who We Are**** TD Securities is one of the world's leading global financial institutions and is the fifth largest bank in North America by branches/stores. Every day, we strive to make every interaction, product, and experience remarkably human and refreshingly simple for over 27 million households and businesses in Canada, the United States and around the world. More than 95,000 TD colleagues bring their skills, talent, and creativity to foster deeper relationships, ensure disciplined execution, and build a simpler, faster banking experience. TD is deeply committed to being a leader in client experience, that is why we believe that all colleagues, no matter where they work, are client facing. Together, we are reimagining what banking can be for our clients, colleagues and communities. **Our Total Rewards Package** Our Total Rewards package reflects the investment we make in our colleagues to help them, and their families achieve their well\-being goals. Total Rewards at TD includes a base salary and several other key plans such as health and well\-being benefits, including medical coverage, paid time off, career development, and reward and recognition programs. ****Additional Information:**** We’re delighted that you’re considering building a career with TD. title: TEAM MEMBER
description: At Sprout, our Team Members are the heart of what we do. You're the face of the company — the one customers come back to see, as much as they come back for the food. From delivering warm, human interactions to helping the team keep the place humming, this is a role for someone who cares about people, pace, and good food. This is more than just a job — it’s a chance to grow, have fun, and be a BIG part of a close\-knit team that loves what they do. **Reporting to:** General Manager **Contract:** Full\-time \& Part\-time available **Pay:** €14\.15–€14\.50 per hour depending on experience and role ****What You’ll Be Doing**** * Versatile Team Player – You’ll jump into different parts of the day, from helping prep fresh ingredients to setting up the service line and keeping the restaurant tidy. * Hospitality Heartbeat – Bring good energy to every interaction and make customers feel genuinely welcome and looked after. * Restaurant Ready – Keep the place sparkling clean, safe, and set up for success throughout the day. * All\-Rounder – Get hands\-on experience across our service line: till, toppings, mix – you’ll learn it all. * Detail\-Oriented – You’ll follow checklists to make sure every dish looks and tastes great. * Menu Confident – Learn our menu so you can guide customers and recommend dishes with confidence. * Safety Champion – Help the team stay on top of food safety, hygiene, and health \& safety standards. ****Who You Are**** * Hospitable – You love making people smile and creating good energy. * Polite \& Kind – Holds the door, jumps in to help, and treats people with respect. * Motivated – You care about doing a good job, no matter what the task is. * Curious – You’re eager to learn and grow. You ask questions and don’t pretend to know it all. * Positive Energy (Oomph!) – You bring great energy into the room. Excited to be here. * Team Player – You’re happiest when you’re helping others. You believe more joy comes from giving than getting. * Values\-Driven – You show up with a good attitude and live the Sprout values. ****What We’re Looking For**** * You don’t need experience for this role, we’ll teach you everything you need to know. What matters most is your attitude and willingness to learn. Here’s what we’re looking for: * Good Level of English \- You can understand instructions and communicate confidently with customers and the team. * Curious \& Willing to Learn – You take feedback well and are keen to grow. * Reliable – You turn up on time and take pride in doing a good job. ****Why Work at Sprout?**** * Great food for you on every shift * Growth opportunities \- we love to promote from within * Team discount across all locations * Well\-being and mental health support * Regular team socials and celebrations * A fun, friendly team that genuinely care. If you’re someone who thrives in a team, loves a bit of buzz, and wants to grow in a company that values people and positivity, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Tendering Engineer
description: Tendering Engineer \- Field Services Location \- Dublin, Ireland What will you do? As part of a Services Business Unit, the role of tender engineer is to: * Support the Sales Team by ensuring we understand the customers commercial and technical requirements and are able to submit a technically compliant offer in line with the customers technical requirements and scope of supply as detailed in their Request for Quotation, or otherwise noted in our commercial and/or technical offers by deviations, exclusions or clarifications. It is also the responsibility of the Tendering team to ensure that any offer submitted to our customer is commercially competitive while staying at or above the required margin levels from the business and has approval from the required Approver and SME’s dependent on the value and complexity of the project or service being offered. * Support the Operations Team to ensure that the scope of supply being offered at tender stage is within their ability to deliver from a scope of supply, resource and competence point of view and costed accordingly. It is also the responsibility of the Tendering team to, identify, remove and mitigate risk where possible and/or allow a sufficient risk provision for risks that can not be removed or mitigated at the tender stage to enable the Operations team to successfully deliver the project and maintain the as tendered margin. Job Specific Requirements: * Close collaboration with the opportunity leader to understand the opportunity requirement / Scope / Specification / Needs and Develop Customer request into a Schneider Electric’s offer by developing a proposal that is in line with the Client requirements in the most cost\-effective manner. * Provide tendering expertise in Maintenance, Repair \& Modernisation for existing installed base and customers for Power Services specifically Low Voltage \& Medium Voltage Electrical Distribution Equipment. * Ensure compliance to internal policies and process requirements. * Prepare a detailed cost analysis based on market and/or bid competitiveness and value proposition. * Coordinate \& work with Factories, Internal Group and External Group to build up a proper service offer responding to specific RFQ requirements. * Lead and be responsible for tender approval review with SME and Business Unit Approvers * Provide accurate and approved tender to opportunity leader within SLA. * Support Sales team by participating in bid clarification. * Communicate and coordinate with all internal stakeholders such as Sales, Finance, Operation, Engineering, purchasing to obtain the optimized offer solution. * Ensure complete and proper hand over of won opportunity to Services Operation team. * Ensure good collaboration across all the company (Cross BUs, Cross Teams) What qualifications will make you successful? * Previous working experience preferably in Sales, Tendering or Project Management of LV \& MV Electrical Distribution Equipment. * Knowledge or experience of installation, testing, commissioning and maintaining LV \& MV Electrical Distribution Equipment used in different market segments and environments. * Able to understand basic electrical network, SLD’s, BOM’s etc. to capture Asset information in order to prepare proposal for works. * Degree in Electrical or Mechanical Engineering or equivalent would be desirable but not essential * Very good communication and interpersonal skills * Proficient in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, etc..) * Fluent in both spoken and written English, * Team player who can work effectively with customers and colleagues What we offer you: * Competitive salary \& Bonus Scheme, * 28 Days Annual Leave \+ Public Holidays, * Private Health Care, * Pension Scheme and Life Assurance * Worldwide Employee Share Ownership Programme, * Various Health \& Wellbeing Support Options, Employee * Assistance Programme. * Cycle to Work Scheme * CareerHub (internal mobility opportunities \+ access to mentors \& projects globally), * Learning Portal and much more. presentation) and tools (Whiteboarding, Slack, Google Slides, Zoom). + **Resource Application** : Continuously runs toward results using the full capabilities of available resources and tools. + **Team Selling** : Aligns with the full capacities of the account team and partners to support the deal and customer success. + **Fluency** in Danish and English**Preferred Qualifications :** ------------------------------ + Sales Methodology education (like **MEDDIC, Challenger, or Value Selling** .) + Ability to develop cases and service requirements, while crafting and leading strategic alliances. + Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced environment. + Track record of consistently achieving or surpassing quota. + Experience will be evaluated based on alignment to the core competencies for the role.**What’s In It For You (Benefits \& Growth)** ============================================= + **Personal Wellbeing** : Private health insurance, life insurance, a retirement savings plan, and a monthly wellness allowance. + **Flexibility** : Flexible time off and leave policies, plus comprehensive parental support programs. + **Career Development** : Access to world\-class AI and sales training, mentorship, and a clear path for promotion within the global Salesforce ecosystem. + **Giving Back** : 7 paid volunteer days per year and donation matching for the causes you care about. + **Perks** : Employee discounts, inclusive resource groups, and a culture that celebrates Equality.**Equality \& Inclusion** ========================= Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer committed to building an inclusive workplace. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and ensure reasonable accommodations are provided throughout the recruitment process where required. **Attachments** --------------- Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form . Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. Posting Statement Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Total Spare Management Administrator
description: **About the Company** MSR\-FSR is an international managed engineering services provider supporting leading organisations in the high\-technology sector. We collaborate with major global clients and are recognised for delivering high\-quality engineering solutions. We offer structured training, career development opportunities, and the chance to work on large\-scale projects across Europe. **Position Overview** We are currently recruiting for a **Total Spare Management Administrator**. This role (after training period is complete) will be a hybrid role of **3 days onsite and 2 days WFH, at Manager discretion.** The Total Spare Management Administrator manages and controls semiconductor manufacturing spares across plants/production lines to ensure equipment uptime. This role focuses on accurate spare part master data, inventory control, spare request fulfilment, stock audits, and readiness planning aligned with semiconductor manufacturing requirements. **Key Responsibilities** * Spare parts management system\*\* (spare module/ERP/CMMS integration), ensuring accurate item setup and lifecycle status. * Maintain complete and correct \*\*critical spares master data\*\*: * Part numbers, alternates, BOM/kit definitions (if used), manufacturer references, compatibility, and substitutes * Lead times, MOQ, storage requirements, and serialisation/traceability rules (if applicable) \- Manage the full spare flow: * Receiving/put\-away confirmation \- inspection/quality checks coordination (as required). * Issuance to maintenance/production teams based on approvals and procedures \- returns, scrap/write\-off processing, and root\-cause support for discrepancies. * Monitor \*\*stock health\*\* for critical equipment: \- min/max levels, reorder points, safety stock, and buffer planning. * Ensure correct part selection for equipment models/lines \- ensure timely fulfilment to support downtime reduction targets. * Conduct and support \*\*cycle counts, audits, and reconciliations\*\*: * Investigate variances and ensure corrective actions are recorded \- Support uptime strategy by identifying and maintaining: * \*\*A/B/C critical spares\*\* classification (or company\-defined criticality tiers) \- consumption trends, slow\-moving/excess stock reporting * Track and report \*\*spare performance metrics\*\* to leadership: \- stock availability/service level, stock\-out causes, turnaround time for requests * Overdue orders, supplier performance inputs (lead time adherence) * Work closely with \*\*Procurement/Supply Chain\*\*: \- trigger replenishment actions * Manage vendor documentation and ensure part data alignment with purchasing * Ensure compliance with semiconductor\-specific requirements. * ESD\-safe handling/storage rules, cleanliness/packing standards when applicable. * Traceability and documentation retention for regulated/critical components. * Maintain controlled access and governance: \- standard approval workflows. * Change management for master data updates. **Requirements*** Diploma/Degree in \*\*Engineering, Supply Chain, Logistics, or Business\*\* (or equivalent experience). * Proven experience in \*\*spares/inventory administration\*\* and/or equipment maintenance support. * Hands\-on experience with \*\*ERP/CMMS or inventory systems\*\* (SAP, Oracle, Maximo, etc.). * Strong data accuracy and reconciliation skills. * Intermediate\-to\-advanced \*\*Excel\*\* (reports, pivot tables, inventory analytics). * Experience in \*\*semiconductor manufacturing\*\* or high\-tech maintenance environments. * Familiarity with \*\*equipment spare criticality, lead\-time planning, and reliability\-driven inventory\*\*. * Knowledge of maintenance/production downtime processes (CMMS workflows). \#\#\# Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) * Inventory accuracy (audit variance %) \- Spare request cycle time (request\-to\-issue) \- Stock\-out frequency and severity (critical spares) * Reorder compliance (on\-time replenishment) \- Reduction in excess/obsolete spares while maintaining availability. **Benefits** **What We Offer** * Comprehensive technical training program. * Opportunities for career growth within an international environment. * Exposure to advanced semiconductor technologies. * A professional and supportive team.

title: Trade Mark Associate (Trainee)
description: **The Role** The role of a Trade Mark Attorney is to assist clients with the registration and protection of their trade marks. Our team of highly experienced attorneys advise on the availability, protection, use and enforcement of trade marks. Our extensive client base ranges from start up ventures to multinational corporations in a wide range of sectors. On the job training and support will be given to enable the successful candidate to qualify as a registered Trade Mark Attorney. **The Candidate** Candidates should have a degree in Law, preferably with an IP module or a modern European language (2:1 equivalent). Applicants will have a logical approach, be capable of working both independently and as part of a team, have sharp analytical skills and excellent oral and written communication skills. In addition, applicants must possess a commitment to quality and attention to detail and also be able to work efficiently and meet deadlines.

title: Trainee Craftsperson (Panel Beater/Spray Painter)
description: **About National Vehicle Distribution (NVD)** National Vehicle Distribution is Ireland’s leading provider of integrated vehicle logistics services. From our state\-of\-the\-art facilities, we support major automotive brands by managing the complete journey of new vehicles—from their arrival in Ireland to their final handover at dealerships. We are committed to quality, efficiency, and developing the next generation of automotive talent. The Opportunity NVD is seeking an enthusiastic and motivated Trainee Craftsperson to join our technical team. This is an excellent entry\-level opportunity to earn while you learn through a structured apprenticeship. You will work with experienced technicians on a variety of brand\-new vehicles, gaining recognised qualifications and building a solid foundation for a long\-term career in the automotive industry. Throughout your apprenticeship, you will learn essential skills in vehicle preparation, enhancement, and quality control within a high\-volume workshop environment. **Successful applicants will be invited to a recruitment day on Wednesday, 8th July at 10am in our Baldonnell Vehicle Preparation Centre.** Key Responsibilities Working under the guidance of senior craftspeople and mentors, you will: * Participate fully in all on\-the\-job training and development activities * Learn to carry out Pre\-Delivery Inspection (PDI) checks in line with manufacturer standards, including fluid levels, tyre pressures, battery health, and mechanical/electrical systems * Assist with and learn accessory installation such as tow bars, roof racks, parking sensors, and in\-car technology * Develop skills in diagnosing and resolving minor transport\-related faults and electronic issues using diagnostic tools * Learn cosmetic enhancement techniques, including minor paint touch\-ups, polishing, and interior/exterior valeting * Maintain a clean, safe, and well\-organised workshop area while learning correct tool usage and care * Complete all tasks to a high standard, following manufacturer specifications and NVD quality procedures * Accurately complete job cards and digital records for all work carried out The Ideal Candidate **Required** * A genuine interest in motor vehicles and mechanical work * A strong motivation to learn and commit to a multi\-year apprenticeship * Good manual dexterity and a logical, methodical approach to problem\-solving * Ability to follow detailed instructions and work safely * Reliable, punctual, and hardworking attitude * Strong communication skills and a collaborative mindset Nice to Have * Leaving Certificate, ideally with a pass in Mathematics or a technical subject (e.g., Engineering, Technology, Technical Drawing) * Pre\-apprenticeship training in motor technology or a related discipline * Practical experience working on vehicles or machinery, even informally * Basic IT skills for using diagnostic and job management systems What We Offer * Structured apprentice pay progression * Clear career pathways, including opportunities to advance to Senior Craftsperson, Master Technician, or Workshop Management roles * Access to modern facilities, the latest diagnostic tools, and new vehicle models * Dedicated mentorship and ongoing support from highly experienced technicians * A comprehensive benefits package, including a pension scheme and employee assistance programme * All required PPE, company uniform, and a contribution towards tooling and driving lesson

title: Trainee Merchandiser
description: Because your perspective shapes trends. Innovate, your way. **Merchandising at Primark** Our merchandising team turn strategic goals into vast opportunities. Here, your insights will maximise our growth as a business and ensure we continue to stay at the forefront in the world of retail fashion. **What You’ll Do As a Trainee Merchandiser** As a Trainee Merchandiser at Primark, you’ll assist the Merchandiser and Assistant Merchandiser in the delivery of our sales, stocks and contribution budgets. You’ll learn about the industry and be given all the tools you need to progress in a Merchandiser role. Primark is a growing business in a competitive industry, with exciting expansion across the UK, Europe, and the US. Your contribution will focus on delivering budgets and line projections, investigating data inaccuracies and forecasting sales. You’ll also have exciting responsibilities relating to products and trends. **What You'll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: * 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. * Flexible Working, opportunity for an early Friday finish, and a subsidised cafeteria. * Primark Perks – Discounts with some local partner business to our offices **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Analytical \- An analytical thinker with an eye for detail and numbers, who also loves working in a team. * Curious \- Investigate and resolve data inaccuracies, report on stock overages and shortages and ensure that current commitment records have been maintained accurately. * Customer focused \- Develop a strong knowledge of our global customer base and use this knowledge to offer outstanding value to our customer, and key recommendations to your team. * Strategically minded \- Act as a self\-starter, working on a range of products trading in multiple regions. As your knowledge of your product and customer grows, you’ll identify opportunities – learning from sales reactions. * Never miss an opportunity \- Use your initiative to identify current and future trading opportunities and propose your recommendations to your team. * Total organisation \- Use your passion for people to build key relationships that will enable you to progress deliveries and shipping documentation, drive stakeholder engagement, and collaborate with your team. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business a over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility requests, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. Our fashion isn’t one\-size\-fits\-all and neither is our culture. Primark promotes equal employment opportunity, we strive to create an inclusive workplace where people can be themselves, access opportunities and thrive together. **REQ ID:** REF3761N

title: Trainee Optical Assistant
description: **Specsavers, Blanchardstown \- Ireland** Trainee Optical Assistant So, you’re a proactive people person, passionate about retail and offering the best customer service? Sounds like you’d be a great fit here. So, if you’ve ever considered a career in optics, this Trainee Optical Assistant role could be perfect for you. As a Trainee Optical Assistant, you don’t need to have previous experience, you just need to be prepared to create an outstanding store environment for both our customers and our team, making sure every patient receives the best care in our stores. We aren’t looking for people with years of experience and heaps of qualifications, just passionate, hard\-workers ready to confidently welcome and help customers as they walk through our doors. And once you’ve found your feet as a Trainee Optical Assistant, you don’t just have to stop there. We have plenty of professional training and development in other business areas, as well as the support of experienced Opticians and Technicians supporting you on your journey. So, whatever you want your career to look like, you can bring it to life at Specsavers. **Our store** Based in the Blanchardstown Shopping Centre, our store has off\-site parking and bike lockups available. With sustainability at the forefront, Specsavers Blanchardstown is a community driven store that actively participates with charities and community initiatives alike. **What’s on Offer?** As well as all the support you need and great training and development opportunities, we have a whole load of benefits on offer for you to enjoy. These include: * You can expect to earn up to €35,000 once passed probation and relevant learning \& development modules completed * 30 days annual leave (Includes Bank Holidays) * Full time * Professional fees paid * Specsavers Perks – our discounted benefits scheme * Annual subscription to the “Headspace” app * Outstanding clinical and professional development opportunities * Support with IP, other higher accreditations and Pathway * Access to latest clinical technology and equipment * Support with CPD points * Regular team events and team building evenings **What we’re looking for?** If this is still sounding like a bit of you, we do need you to have a few skills to get started as a Trainee Optical Assistant. These include: * Passion for providing excellent customer service and making customers’ lives easier * Great teamwork * A good initiative * Confidence in using IT systems * Excellent communication and listening skills * Prepared to step out of your comfort zone and try something new * Able to receive feedback and be open to development, knowing we’ll be there to support and guide you throughout your process (with your openness and our guidance, your opportunities will be endless). **Find out more** If you’ve got all these skills, we’d love for you to apply, but there’s also some desirable experience that could set you apart from the competition if you have it. **This includes** * Previous experience in a fast\-paced customer service environment * Experience in optics or Audiology business * Basic knowledge/experience of optical and/or Audiology terminology. Please be aware you may be requsted to complete a short video as part of the interview process! **Checked all the boxes? Now’s the perfect time to apply!**

title: Training and Implementation Executive
description: At **Alkimii** , we’re on a mission to transform how hospitality businesses manage their people and property operations. Our platform helps hotels simplify operations, improve communication across teams, and run their businesses more efficiently. As we continue to grow, we’re looking for a **Training \& Implementation Executive** to help new customers successfully launch and adopt the Alkimii platform. This is a great opportunity for someone with **who enjoys training, learning new technology and helping teams adopt new systems.** You will initially support implementations alongside experienced team members and progressively take ownership of customer onboarding projects as you grow in the role. **What you’ll be doing** As part of our **Training \& Implementation Team** , you will help guide new customers through their onboarding journey and ensure they feel confident using Alkimii in their day\-to\-day operations. In this role you will: • Support the implementation of the Alkimii platform for new customers across Ireland and the UK • Work closely with customers to understand their operational processes and help configure the system to meet their needs • Deliver training sessions (online and on\-site) to help teams learn how to use the platform effectively • Assist with onboarding activities including preparation, configuration and follow\-up with customers • Provide ongoing support during the early stages of adoption to ensure a smooth transition • Collaborate with the Customer Success and Product teams to share feedback and improve the onboarding experience As you grow in the role, you will gradually take **greater ownership of implementation projects and customer onboarding journeys** . **What success looks like in your first months** • You build a strong understanding of the Alkimii platform and how hospitality teams use it • You confidently support onboarding sessions and training with customers • Customers feel supported and comfortable adopting the system • You contribute ideas to improve the onboarding experience and internal processes **We think you’ll thrive in this role if you:** • Have experience working in hospitality operations, management or eduation. • Enjoy helping people learn and adopt new tools • Are comfortable speaking with customers and delivering presentations • Are organised and able to manage multiple tasks and priorities • Are curious, proactive and eager to develop your career in a growing SaaS company **Requirements** • Strong computer and technical skills • Excellent communication and presentation skills • Fluent written and spoken English • Eligibility to work full\-time in Ireland • Full clean driving licence • Flexibility to travel across Ireland and occasionally to the UK **What can you expect from us:** 💰Full time competitive salary 🩺 Wellbeing programs, learning and growth opportunities 🧘 ♀️ Flexible working week, because we take seriously the productivity and wellbeing of our team 🏝️ 25 days vacation 🚗 Mileage and expenses 🚀 You will work on meaningful and challenging tasks in a relaxed yet supportive environment. 🙋 4,000 Employee Referral Program 🌅 Work in a beautiful building located on Skerries Harbour overlooking the pier! we get the best sunsets! 👯 Quarterly team meet\-ups \& annual gathering 💲Clevercards \- Employee Benefits **Why join Alkimii?** We’re a team that cares deeply about our customers and about each other. Our culture is built on kindness, curiosity and ownership, and we’re always looking for ways to improve how we work and how we support hospitality teams around the world. If you enjoy solving problems, helping people and being part of a growing company where your ideas matter, we’d love to hear from you. *Before you apply, we feel it’s important that you know a bit more about us…* We: * Enjoy being up early enough to see the sun rise * Appreciate a good barista coffee * Discipline ourselves with morning workouts * Feed our curiosity with a good book * Don’t take ourselves too seriously * Constantly ask ourselves how we can make it easier for our customers * Love long walks on the beach (yes, really!) All sounding good? Check out what your new commute might look like: https://goo.gl/maps/cf5LPcJmCBBPYZFR8

title: Warehouse Operative
description: T **J O' Mahony Bluebell,** John F Kennedy Road, John F Kennedy Industrial Estate, Bluebell, Co Dublin, D12 KR94, part of HPC Sales Ltd., Irelands' leading Builders Providers and Home Improvement/DIY Store, is looking for a Full\-Time, Permanent, Warehouse Operative to join our Team **Job Purpose:** Warehouse Operative **.** **Duties and Responsibilities:** Pick and complete sales orders, wrap deliveries, complete warehouse paperwork for each dispatch. Unload, record and put away of stocks and deliveries Ensure all Health and Safety standards are always adhered to in line with Company policy. Load and unload lorries in a safe and efficient manner Move and stack materials Using correct manual handling techniques. Perform general warehouse duties in order to maintain a smooth operation Checking that finished product packs are closed/sealed properly and that labels are in the correct position Delivering product to customers by van may also be a requirement of the position **Requirements:** Good communication and interpersonal skills Computer Skills Friendly, helpful attitude Have full reach and counterbalance forklift licences **Benefits:** Career progression Bonus Scheme Bike to Work Scheme Free On\-Site Parking **Hours:** 40 hours per week (Monday \- Saturday) *Home Project Centre Sales Ltd. is an equal opportunities employer.*

title: Work Ready Graduate Programme: UX / UI / Learning Sciences Graduates
description: **Graduates in User Experience, Learner Experience, Learning Sciences and Instructional Design \- come work with us!** **Learnovate Work Ready Graduate Programme** * Edtech Industry work placement * Training \& One\-to\-one mentoring * Laptop provided * Salary: €38,000 \+ annual leave * Building networks in industry and innovation ecosystems * Duration: Full\-time, 1 year (August 2026 – August 2027\) \*\*\*\* Please note: **Only expressions of interest submitted via the google form link** below will be considered for application to the programme. Please do not apply via any other methods. \*\*\*\* Apply here https://forms.gle/LSFDNoGeXho1asoj9 **About Learnovate** Learnovate is an industry lead research and innovation centre focused on the Future of Work and Learning supported by Enterprise Ireland, the IDA and hosted by Trinity College Dublin. Our team has rich expertise in learning science, technology, user\-centric product design and customer\-centric innovation for over twelve years. We are excited to announce that applications are open for Year 3 of our Work Ready Graduate Programme, which aims to provide opportunities in the learning technology industry. What is the Learnovate Work Ready Graduate Programme? Graduates participate in a year\-long work placement hosted by a partner company and supported by the Learnovate team, employed by Trinity College Dublin. During the year the graduate will work in the company on a project focused on or involving a learning technology solution supporting the learner in either a corporate learning or a formal education environment. Throughout the 12 month placement the graduate is hosted by one of Learnovate’s partner companies and supported by a mentor in Learnovate, along with the following opportunities: * Formal skills training, networking and engagement opportunities * Business skills training for example accredited Project Management \& Scrum * One\-to\-one mentoring with Learnovate senior researcher * Access to the latest knowledge and technologies in regards to the future of work and learning * Hands\-on experience to implement technical solutions that support the learner in work and across a variety of learning contexts * Conference and events attendance **Benefits:** * Industry work placement * Structured training and one\-to\-one mentoring * Laptop provided * Networks in industry and innovation ecosystems * Salary: €38,000 \+ annual leave **Eligibility Criteria:** * Masters Degree is desirable \- a Level 8 degree is acceptable in disciplines relevant to: * Learning sciences * Education * Learner experience/user experience design * Interest in/focus on: * Education * User and Learner Experience * Instructional Design * Eligible to work in Ireland (any visa required to work must be in place at the time of application) **The Programme includes:** * Formal training will include skills training, networking and engagement opportunities. * Informal training facilitated by the experience of participation in the Learnovate multi\-disciplinary team activities and exposure to the innovation methodologies in use. * Access to the latest knowledge and techniques within the current technical landscape in regards to the future of work and learning. * Thorough grounding and training in modern business and software design best practices * Hands\-on experience to implement technical solutions that support the learner in work and across a variety of learning contexts. If you fulfil the eligibility criteria and would like to be considered for participation in this programme, please fill out the following form for more information. Depending on the placement company, there may be opportunities for hybrid and remote working. \*\*\*\* Please note: Only expressions of interest submitted via the google form link below will be considered for application to the programme. Please do not apply via any other methods. \*\*\*\* Apply here https://forms.gle/LSFDNoGeXho1asoj9 This is a 12 month placement, commencing in August, 2026 For any queries email gradapplications@learnovatecentre.org

title: Workday Business Analyst
description: **We are open to this being fixed\-term employment or day rate contract. The project is for 6 months.** **About the role** The Business Analyst role will support optimisation of our Workday platform and delivery of Workday's Advanced Compensation module through improved processes, configuration, and governance across HR, Reward, and technical teams. This is a 6\-month contract reporting into our HR Master Data Lead. The role can be performed remotely or in one of our office locations (hybrid options available). **Key Responsibilities** * Supporting Workday optimisation initiatives, identifying opportunities to streamline functionality and improve user experience. * Facilitating workshops, process mapping, and requirements gathering with HR, Reward and business stakeholders. * Translating business requirements into Workday functional specifications, user stories, and acceptance criteria. * Implementing automated solutions that resolve or reduce manual processes and align with Workday best practice. * Supporting the creation, embedding and maintenance of HR data governance, including data definitions, controls, data quality frameworks and change governance. * Developing and maintaining process documentation, including process flows, SOPs, configuration guides and change logs. * Supporting UAT planning and execution, including test script creation and defect management. **About you** * Proven hands‑on experience with Workday HCM, including configuration, tenant review and troubleshooting. * Experience implementing or enhancing Workday Advanced Compensation module (merit, bonus, stock). * Strong capability in process improvement and understanding end‑to‑end HR process flows. * Skilled at working with technical teams to ensure accurate configuration, integration and reporting outcomes. * Understanding of HR processes, including Core HR, Talent, Compensation and data governance. * Ability to translate business needs into clear functional specifications. * Strong documentation, analytical and problem‑solving skills. **What Success Looks Like** * Optimised Workday processes that deliver a smoother, more intuitive experience for HR and the business. * Well defined, clearly articulated requirements and documentation that support high‑quality system changes. * A successful rollout of Advanced Compensation, with engaged stakeholders and minimal defects. * Strong governance in place, with clear data definitions, quality controls and change processes. * HR teams confident in new processes and functionality due to effective training and knowledge transfer. * Continuous improvement embedded, with feedback loops that drive ongoing Workday enhancements. **About Inizio** At Inizio, we're a global team driven by a shared purpose: to reimagine health. Formed in 2022 through the coming together of Ashfield and Huntsworth, we unite decades of experience with cutting\-edge capabilities to deliver impactful solutions across the commercialization journey \- from discovery to post\-launch. Our global network of over 10,000 experts operates across 50 countries, supporting clients with strategic insight, medical excellence, creative engagement, and real\-world delivery. We work with the world's leading health and life sciences businesses to solve complex challenges and drive meaningful outcomes for our clients and patients. What sets Inizio apart is our people \- and we believe everyone should have the opportunity to thrive. Here, you're encouraged to reimagine your possibilities: to grow, to be curious and to make real change happen. It's a place where your individuality is valued, your development is supported, and your ambition is celebrated.
